generatedSentence: In the interior of the decaying bridge facility, a faint red light glows, hinting at a system ready to unlock a secret. Our subject, a scruffy nerd operating under an alias, was deeply interested in the cryptic image projected onto an acrylic screen. He had an openminded approach to the folders of nonfiction data, eager to verify the truth. His personality was marked by an intense affinity for historical elements, his neurons constantly processing. He felt a profound longing for understanding, even as dull headaches throbbed in his isolated homes. His work was his output, an outline of forgotten memory. Suddenly, a berserker horde initiated a surprise attack, their leader a gruff, overweight mercenary with a pejorative snarl. Security systems failed with rapid logoffs, creating a flooding scenario of enemy combatants. The nerd, underestimated by his foes, executed a gymnastic vertical evasion. He knew this wasn't a generic skirmish; it was a targeted faceoff, their wanton destruction making his stomach tire. He grabbed a charger, a matchsticks-sized device, ready for the inevitable breaking of the calm, feeling the mortification of being caught off guard. As the mercenaries, a fearful migration from a mountain village, advanced, the nerd became a kickass legend, employing an intoxicating lubricant on the slippery cement floor. He used a flashbang and a series of grenades, the optical flares dazzling his foes, who were stuck in a tight formation. He saw a grotesque skeleton, a grim reminder, and a large elephant carved from vegetal fibre, a sacred idol, crumble under their drill. He performed a precise analysis, identifying the vertex and vector of the enemy’s thrust. He knew this was the climax, the ultimate test. With the last enemy done, the nerd, looking like an elegant veejay, surveyed the aftermath; a makeshift tourniquet on his arm was no big deal. The outcome, though messy with gunky residue and grains of sandstorm rime, allowed him to finally induction the ancient system. He smiled, thinking of holidays and a coconut drink, having won the ballot against chaos. No longer a noun, but an adverb of action, he saw a path to warmer, safer homes. His partner, the first baseman of their small team, would be proud; their goal to open the toilet-shaped data vault was now clear.
panelSentences: In the interior of the decaying bridge facility, a faint red light glows, hinting at a system ready to unlock a secret. Our subject, a scruffy nerd operating under an alias, was deeply interested in the cryptic image projected onto an acrylic screen. He had an openminded approach to the folders of nonfiction data, eager to verify the truth. His personality was marked by an intense affinity for historical elements, his neurons constantly processing. He felt a profound longing for understanding, even as dull headaches throbbed in his isolated homes. His work was his output, an outline of forgotten memory.

Suddenly, a berserker horde initiated a surprise attack, their leader a gruff, overweight mercenary with a pejorative snarl. Security systems failed with rapid logoffs, creating a flooding scenario of enemy combatants. The nerd, underestimated by his foes, executed a gymnastic vertical evasion. He knew this wasn't a generic skirmish; it was a targeted faceoff, their wanton destruction making his stomach tire. He grabbed a charger, a matchsticks-sized device, ready for the inevitable breaking of the calm, feeling the mortification of being caught off guard.

As the mercenaries, a fearful migration from a mountain village, advanced, the nerd became a kickass legend, employing an intoxicating lubricant on the slippery cement floor. He used a flashbang and a series of grenades, the optical flares dazzling his foes, who were stuck in a tight formation. He saw a grotesque skeleton, a grim reminder, and a large elephant carved from vegetal fibre, a sacred idol, crumble under their drill. He performed a precise analysis, identifying the vertex and vector of the enemy’s thrust. He knew this was the climax, the ultimate test.

With the last enemy done, the nerd, looking like an elegant veejay, surveyed the aftermath; a makeshift tourniquet on his arm was no big deal. The outcome, though messy with gunky residue and grains of sandstorm rime, allowed him to finally induction the ancient system. He smiled, thinking of holidays and a coconut drink, having won the ballot against chaos. No longer a noun, but an adverb of action, he saw a path to warmer, safer homes. His partner, the first baseman of their small team, would be proud; their goal to open the toilet-shaped data vault was now clear.
rawPanelSentences: In the interior of the decaying bridge facility, a faint red light glows, hinting at a system ready to unlock a secret. Our subject, a scruffy nerd operating under an alias, was deeply interested in the cryptic image projected onto an acrylic screen. He had an openminded approach to the folders of nonfiction data, eager to verify the truth. His personality was marked by an intense affinity for historical elements, his neurons constantly processing. He felt a profound longing for understanding, even as dull headaches throbbed in his isolated homes. His work was his output, an outline of forgotten memory.

Suddenly, a berserker horde initiated a surprise attack, their leader a gruff, overweight mercenary with a pejorative snarl. Security systems failed with rapid logoffs, creating a flooding scenario of enemy combatants. The nerd, underestimated by his foes, executed a gymnastic vertical evasion. He knew this wasn't a generic skirmish; it was a targeted faceoff, their wanton destruction making his stomach tire. He grabbed a charger, a matchsticks-sized device, ready for the inevitable breaking of the calm, feeling the mortification of being caught off guard.

As the mercenaries, a fearful migration from a mountain village, advanced, the nerd became a kickass legend, employing an intoxicating lubricant on the slippery cement floor. He used a flashbang and a series of grenades, the optical flares dazzling his foes, who were stuck in a tight formation. He saw a grotesque skeleton, a grim reminder, and a large elephant carved from vegetal fibre, a sacred idol, crumble under their drill. He performed a precise analysis, identifying the vertex and vector of the enemy’s thrust. He knew this was the climax, the ultimate test.

With the last enemy done, the nerd, looking like an elegant veejay, surveyed the aftermath; a makeshift tourniquet on his arm was no big deal. The outcome, though messy with gunky residue and grains of sandstorm rime, allowed him to finally induction the ancient system. He smiled, thinking of holidays and a coconut drink, having won the ballot against chaos. No longer a noun, but an adverb of action, he saw a path to warmer, safer homes. His partner, the first baseman of their small team, would be proud; their goal to open the toilet-shaped data vault was now clear.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: a lean, agile figure with glowing blue cybernetic eyes and a sleek, red-and-black armored jumpsuit. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist in a dilapidated, glowing interior, staring at an ancient holographic image, low camera angle. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist mid-air in a dynamic gymnastic evade, as a horde of gruff mercenaries breach a security checkpoint, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist throwing grenades, their optical flares bursting, illuminating a large elephant idol amidst combat, dynamic action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, arm bandaged, standing victorious over defeated foes, a digital system interface now active, medium shot.

definitions.definition: A small explosive device, designed to be thrown by hand or launched from a grenade launcher.
A pomegranate.
A charge similar to a fireball, and made of a disc-shaped bomb shell, but with only one set of flames at the top.
An unattractive girl.
The state of a glowing object.
The condition of being passionate or having warm feelings.
The brilliance or warmth of color in an environment or on a person (especially one's face).
definitions.example: He had a bright red glow on his face.

definitions.definition: To give off light from heat or to emit light as if heated.
definitions.example: The fire was still glowing after ten hours.

definitions.definition: To radiate some emotional quality like light.
definitions.example: The zealots glowed with religious fervor.

definitions.definition: To gaze especially passionately at something.
To radiate thermal heat.
definitions.example: After their workout, the gymnasts' faces were glowing red.

definitions.definition: To shine brightly and steadily.
definitions.example: The new baby's room glows with bright, loving colors.

definitions.definition: To make hot; to flush.
To feel hot; to have a burning sensation, as of the skin, from friction, exercise, etc.; to burn.
The act of unlocking something.
To undo or open a lock or something locked by, for example, turning a key, or selecting a combination.
definitions.example: I unlocked the door and walked in.

definitions.definition: To obtain access to something.
definitions.example: I unlocked the dictionary article so I could edit it.

definitions.definition: To disclose or reveal previously unknown knowledge.
definitions.example: The discovery of a clue unlocked the mystery.

definitions.definition: To be or become unfastened or unrestrained.
To engage the attention of; to awaken interest in; to excite emotion or passion in, in behalf of a person or thing.
definitions.example: Action films don't really interest me.

definitions.definition: To be concerned with or engaged in; to affect; to concern; to excite.
To cause or permit to share.
Having or showing interest.
definitions.example: I'm very interested in going to see that play.

definitions.definition: Motivated by considerations of self-interest; self-serving.
Owning a share of a company.
An optical or other representation of a real object; a graphic; a picture.
definitions.example: The Bible forbids the worship of graven images.

definitions.definition: A mental picture of something not real or not present.
A statue or idol.
A file that contains all information needed to produce a live working copy. (See disk image and image copy.)
definitions.example: Most game console emulators do not come with any ROM images for copyright reasons.

definitions.definition: A characteristic of a person, group or company etc., style, manner of dress, how one is, or wishes to be, perceived by others.
Something mapped to by a function.
definitions.example: The number 6 is the image of 3 under f that is defined as f(x) = 2x.

definitions.definition: The subset of a codomain comprising those elements that are images of something.
A form of interference: a weaker "copy" of a strong signal that occurs at a different frequency.
Show; appearance; cast.
To represent by an image or symbol; to portray.
To reflect, mirror.
To create an image of.
To create a complete backup copy of a file system or other entity.
The inside of a building, container, cavern, or other enclosed structure.
The inside regions of a country, distanced from the borders or coasts.
definitions.example: Sir Richard Burton explored far into the African interior.

definitions.definition: The set of all interior points of a set.
Within any limits, enclosure, or substance; inside; internal; inner.
definitions.example: the interior apartments of a house; the interior surface of a hollow ball

definitions.definition: Remote from the limits, frontier, or shore; inland.
definitions.example: the interior parts of a region or country

definitions.definition: A construction or natural feature that spans a divide.
An arch or superstructure.
A connection, real or abstract.
Any of several electrical devices that measure characteristics such as impedance and inductance by balancing different parts of a circuit
A low wall or vertical partition in the fire chamber of a furnace, for deflecting flame, etc.; a bridge wall.
The situation where a lone rider or small group of riders closes the space between them and the rider or group in front.
A solid crust of undissolved salt in a water softener.
To be or make a bridge over something.
definitions.example: With enough cable, we can bridge this gorge.

definitions.definition: To span as if with a bridge.
definitions.example: The two groups were able to bridge their differences.

definitions.definition: To transition from one piece or section of music to another without stopping.
(communication) To connect two or more computer buses, networks etc. with a bridge.
To go to the bridge position.
The system that provides support to an organism, internal and made up of bones and cartilage in vertebrates, external in some other animals.
An anthropomorphic representation of a skeleton.
definitions.example: She dressed up as a skeleton for Halloween.

definitions.definition: A very thin person.
definitions.example: She lost so much weight while she was ill that she became a skeleton.

definitions.definition: The central core of something that gives shape to the entire structure.
definitions.example: The skeleton of the organisation is essentially the same as it was ten years ago, but many new faces have come and gone.

definitions.definition: A frame that provides support to a building or other construction.
A client-helper procedure that communicates with a stub.
definitions.example: In remote method invocation, the client helper is a ‘stub’ and the service helper is a ‘skeleton’.

definitions.definition: The vertices and edges of a polyhedron, taken collectively.
A very thin form of light-faced type.
(attributive) Reduced to a minimum or bare essentials.
To reduce to a skeleton; to skin; to skeletonize
To minimize
A hand grenade that produces a very bright flash and a loud explosion, but no shrapnel and minimal explosive force, to disorient the target.
An organizer that papers are kept in, usually with an index tab, to be stored as a single unit in a filing cabinet.
definitions.example: I keep all my schoolwork in a yellow folder.

definitions.definition: A virtual container in a computer's file system, in which files and other folders may be stored. The files and subfolders in a folder are usually related.
definitions.example: My essays are in the folder named "Essays".

definitions.definition: A machine or person that folds things.
A folding knife.
Willing to consider new and different ideas or opinions.
The first batter in the batting order.
definitions.example: Jones has been the team's lead off hitter for three years.

definitions.definition: The first batter of an inning.
definitions.example: The lead off hitter for the sixth inning is Jones.

definitions.definition: The short distance that a player stands away from their current base.
Beginning something by leading the way.
The highest point of something.
The highest surface on the skull.
The common point of the two rays of the angle, or its equivalent structure in polyhedra (meeting of edges) and higher order polytopes.
A point on the curve with a local minimum or maximum of curvature.
One of the elements of a graph joined or not by edges to other vertices.
A point in 3D space, usually given in terms of its Cartesian coordinates.
The point where the surface of a lens crosses the optical axis.
An interaction point.
The point where the prime vertical meets the ecliptic in the western hemisphere of a natal chart.
A sharp downward point opposite a crotch, as in the letters "V" and "W" but not "Y".
A directed quantity, one with both magnitude and direction; the signed difference between two points.
An ordered tuple representing a directed quantity or the signed difference between two points.
Any member of a (generalized) vector space.
definitions.example: The vectors in {\mathbb Q}[X] are the single-variable polynomials with rational coefficients: one is \textstyle x^{42}+\frac1{137}x-1.

definitions.definition: A chosen course or direction for motion, as of an aircraft.
A carrier of a disease-causing agent.
(by extension) A person or entity that passes along an urban legend or other meme.
A recurring psychosocial issue that stimulates growth and development in the personality.
The way in which the eyes are drawn across the visual text. The trail that a book cover can encourage the eyes to follow from certain objects to others.

(operating systems) A memory address containing the address of a code entry point, usually one which is part of a table and often one that is dereferenced and jumped to during the execution of an interrupt.
A one-dimensional array.
A graphical representation using outlines; vector graphics.
definitions.example: a vector image

definitions.definition: A DNA molecule used to carry genetic information from one organism into another.
To set (particularly an aircraft) on a course toward a selected point.
To redirect to a vector, or code entry point.
Any vegetable organism.
Capable of growth and reproduction, but not feeling or reason (often opposed to sensible and rational).
Pertaining to vegetables or plants.
Having a grassy, herbaceous taste.
A tightly-compressed bandage used to stop bleeding by stopping the flow of blood through a large artery in a limb.
Any of several similar methods of clamping components into position.
A turnstile.
(auxiliary) A syntactic marker.
To perform; to execute.
definitions.example: All you ever do is surf the Internet. What will you do this afternoon?

definitions.definition: To cause, make (someone) (do something).
To suffice.
definitions.example: it’s not the best broom, but it will have to do;  this will do me, thanks.

definitions.definition: To be reasonable or acceptable.
definitions.example: It simply will not do to have dozens of children running around such a quiet event.

definitions.definition: (ditransitive) To have (as an effect).
definitions.example: The fresh air did him some good.

definitions.definition: To fare, perform (well or poorly).
definitions.example: Our relationship isn't doing very well;  how do you do?

definitions.definition: (chiefly in questions) To have as one's job.
definitions.example: What does Bob do? — He's a plumber.

definitions.definition: To perform the tasks or actions associated with (something).
definitions.example: "Don't forget to do your report" means something quite different depending on whether you're a student or a programmer.

definitions.definition: To cook.
definitions.example: I'll just do some eggs.

definitions.definition: To travel in, to tour, to make a circuit of.
definitions.example: Let’s do New York also.

definitions.definition: To treat in a certain way.
To work for or on, by way of caring for, looking after, preparing, cleaning, keeping in order, etc.
To act or behave in a certain manner; to conduct oneself.
To spend (time) in jail. (See also do time)
definitions.example: I did five years for armed robbery.

definitions.definition: To impersonate or depict.
definitions.example: They really laughed when he did Clinton, with a perfect accent and a leer.

definitions.definition: (with 'a' and the name of a person, place, event, etc.) To copy or emulate the actions or behaviour that is associated with the person or thing mentioned.
definitions.example: He did a Henry VIII and got married six times.

definitions.definition: To kill.
To deal with for good and all; to finish up; to undo; to ruin; to do for.
To punish for a misdemeanor.
definitions.example: He got done for speeding.

definitions.definition: To have sex with. (See also do it)
To cheat or swindle.
definitions.example: That guy just did me out of two hundred bucks!

definitions.definition: To convert into a certain form; especially, to translate.
definitions.example: the novel has just been done into English;  I'm going to do this play into a movie

definitions.definition: To finish.
To work as a domestic servant (with for).
(auxiliary) Used to form the present progressive of verbs.
To cash or to advance money for, as a bill or note.
(ditransitive) To make or provide.
definitions.example: Could you do me a burger with mayonnaise instead of ketchup?

definitions.definition: To injure (one's own body part).
To take drugs.
definitions.example: I do cocaine.

definitions.definition: (in the form be doing [somewhere]) To exist with a purpose or for a reason.
definitions.example: What's that car doing in our swimming pool?

definitions.definition: (of food) Ready, fully cooked.
definitions.example: As soon as the potatoes are done we can sit down and eat.

definitions.definition: Having completed or finished an activity.
definitions.example: He pushed his empty plate away, sighed and pronounced "I am done."

definitions.definition: Being exhausted or fully spent.
definitions.example: When the water is done we will only be able to go on for a few days.

definitions.definition: Without hope or prospect of completion or success.
definitions.example: He is done, after three falls there is no chance he will be able to finish.

definitions.definition: Fashionable, socially acceptable, tasteful.
definitions.example: I can't believe he just walked up and spoke to her like that, those kind of things just aren't done!

definitions.definition: A gymnast.
Pertaining to gymnastics.
Pertaining to the gymnasia (ancient Greek schools).
definitions.example: gymnastic dialogues

definitions.definition: A product sold under a generic name.
A wine that is a blend of several wines, or made from a blend of several grape varieties.
(grammar) A term that specifies neither male nor female.
Very comprehensive; pertaining or appropriate to large classes or groups as opposed to specific.
definitions.example: "Shrimp" is the generic name for a number of species of sea creature.

definitions.definition: Lacking in precision, often in an evasive fashion; vague; imprecise.
(of a product or drug) Not having a brand name.
Of or relating to a taxonomic genus.
Relating to gender.
(grammar) Specifying neither masculine nor feminine; epicene.
(Of program code) Written so as to operate on any data type, the type required being passed as a parameter.
(of a point) Having coordinates that are algebraically independent over the base field.
To stupefy by doping with chemical substances such as alcohol.
To excite to enthusiasm or madness.
(of a substance) Able to intoxicate; an intoxicant.
Very exciting and stimulating, like alcohol or some other stimulant.
To point out; to discover; to direct to a knowledge of; to show; to make known.
definitions.example: The guard blew his whistle to indicate imminent departure.

definitions.definition: To show or manifest by symptoms; to point to as the proper remedies.
definitions.example: Great prostration of strength indicates the use of stimulants.

definitions.definition: To signal in a vehicle the desire to turn right or left.
To investigate the condition or power of, as of steam engine, by means of an indicator.
To separate into two or more pieces, to fracture or crack, by a process that cannot easily be reversed for reassembly.
definitions.example: If the vase falls to the floor, it might break.

definitions.definition: To divide (something, often money) into smaller units.
definitions.example: Can you break a hundred-dollar bill for me?

definitions.definition: To cause (a person or animal) to lose spirit or will; to crush the spirits of.
definitions.example: Her child's death broke Angela.

definitions.definition: To be crushed, or overwhelmed with sorrow or grief.
definitions.example: My heart is breaking.

definitions.definition: To interrupt; to destroy the continuity of; to dissolve or terminate.
definitions.example: I had won four games in a row, but now you've broken my streak of luck.

definitions.definition: To ruin financially.
definitions.example: The recession broke some small businesses.

definitions.definition: To violate, to not adhere to.
definitions.example: He broke his vows by cheating on his wife.

definitions.definition: (of a fever) To pass the most dangerous part of the illness; to go down, in terms of temperature.
definitions.example: Susan's fever broke at about 3 AM, and the doctor said the worst was over.

definitions.definition: (of a spell of settled weather) To end.
definitions.example: The forecast says the hot weather will break by midweek.

definitions.definition: (of a storm) To begin; to end.
definitions.example: Around midday the storm broke, and the afternoon was calm and sunny.

definitions.definition: (of morning, dawn, day etc.) To arrive.
definitions.example: Morning has broken.

definitions.definition: To render (a game) unchallenging by altering its rules or exploiting loopholes or weaknesses in them in a way that gives a player an unfair advantage.
definitions.example: Changing the rules to let white have three extra queens would break chess.

definitions.definition: To stop, or to cause to stop, functioning properly or altogether.
definitions.example: Did you two break the trolley by racing with it?

definitions.definition: To cause (a barrier) to no longer bar.
definitions.example: break a seal

definitions.definition: To destroy the arrangement of; to throw into disorder; to pierce.
definitions.example: The cavalry were not able to break the British squares.

definitions.definition: (of a wave of water) To collapse into surf, after arriving in shallow water.
To burst forth; to make its way; to come into view.
To interrupt or cease one's work or occupation temporarily.
definitions.example: Let's break for lunch.

definitions.definition: To interrupt (a fall) by inserting something so that the falling object does not (immediately) hit something else beneath.
definitions.example: He survived the jump out the window because the bushes below broke his fall.

definitions.definition: To disclose or make known an item of news, etc.
definitions.example: I don't know how to break this to you, but your cat is not coming back.

definitions.definition: (of a sound) To become audible suddenly.
To change a steady state abruptly.
definitions.example: His coughing broke the silence.

definitions.definition: To suddenly become.
definitions.example: The arrest was standard, when suddenly the suspect broke ugly.

definitions.definition: Of a male voice, to become deeper at puberty.
Of a voice, to alter in type due to emotion or strain: in men generally to go up, in women sometimes to go down; to crack.
definitions.example: His voice breaks when he gets emotional.

definitions.definition: To surpass or do better than (a specific number), to do better than (a record), setting a new record.
definitions.example: He broke the men's 100-meter record.

definitions.definition: (most often in the passive tense) To demote, to reduce the military rank of.
To end (a connection), to disconnect.
definitions.example: I couldn't hear a thing he was saying, so I broke the connection and called him back.

definitions.definition: (of an emulsion) To demulsify.
To counter-attack
To lay open, as a purpose; to disclose, divulge, or communicate.
To become weakened in constitution or faculties; to lose health or strength.
To fail in business; to become bankrupt.
To destroy the strength, firmness, or consistency of.
definitions.example: to break flax

definitions.definition: To destroy the official character and standing of; to cashier; to dismiss.
To make an abrupt or sudden change; to change the gait.
definitions.example: to break into a run or gallop

definitions.definition: To fall out; to terminate friendship.
To terminate the execution of a program before normal completion.
To suspend the execution of a program during debugging so that the state of the program can be investigated.
Financially ruined, bankrupt.
Without any money, penniless.
definitions.example: dead broke; flat broke

definitions.definition: Broken.
definitions.example: If it ain't broke, don't fix it.

definitions.definition: Demoted, deprived of a commission.
definitions.example: He was broke and rendered unfit to serve His Majesty at sea.

definitions.definition: An attempt to cause damage, injury to, or death of opponent or enemy.
An attempt to detract from the worth or credibility of, a person, position, idea, object, or thing, by physical, verbal, emotional, or other assault.
definitions.example: They claimed the censorship of the article was an attack on free speech.

definitions.definition: A time in which one attacks; the offence of a battle.
definitions.example: The army timed their attack to coincide with the local celebrations.

definitions.definition: (by extension) The beginning of active operations on anything.
definitions.example: Having washed the plates from dinner, I made an attack on the laundry.

definitions.definition: An attempt to exploit a vulnerability in a computer system.
Collectively, the bowlers of a cricket side.
Any contact with the ball other than a serve or block which sends the ball across the plane of the net.
The three attackmen on the field or all the attackmen of a team.
The sudden onset of a disease or condition.
definitions.example: I've had an attack of the flu.

definitions.definition: An active episode of a chronic or recurrent disease.
The onset of a musical note, particularly with respect to the strength (and duration) of that onset.

(audio) The amount of time it takes for the volume of an audio signal to go from zero to maximum level (e.g. an audio waveform representing a snare drum hit would feature a very fast attack, whereas that of a wave washing to shore would feature a slow attack).
To apply violent force to someone or something.
definitions.example: This species of snake will only attack humans if it feels threatened.

definitions.definition: To aggressively challenge a person, idea, etc., with words (particularly in newspaper headlines, because it typesets into less space than "criticize" or similar).
definitions.example: She published an article attacking the recent pay cuts.

definitions.definition: To begin to affect; to act upon injuriously or destructively; to begin to decompose or waste.
To deal with something in a direct way; to set to work upon.
definitions.example: I attacked the meal with a hearty appetite.

definitions.definition: To aim balls at the batsman’s wicket.
To set a field, or bowl in a manner designed to get wickets.
To bat aggressively, so as to score runs quickly.
To move forward in an active attempt to score a point, as opposed to trying not to concede.
To accelerate quickly in an attempt to get ahead of the other riders.
The condition of not being threatened, especially physically, psychologically, emotionally, or financially.
Something that secures.
An organization or department responsible for providing security by enforcing laws, rules, and regulations as well as maintaining order.
Something that secures the fulfillment of an obligation or law.
Freedom from apprehension.
(often used in plural) A tradeable financial asset, such as a share of stock.W
Proof of ownership of stocks, bonds or other investment instruments.
Property etc. temporarily relinquished to guarantee repayment of a loan.
A guarantee.
Carelessness; negligence.
Likely to be affected by or to experience something.
definitions.example: He's subject to sneezing fits.

definitions.definition: Conditional upon.
definitions.example: The local board sets local policy, subject to approval from the State Board.

definitions.definition: Placed or situated under; lying below, or in a lower situation.
Placed under the power of another; owing allegiance to a particular sovereign or state.
A strong wind carrying clouds of sand and dust through the air.
Ice formed by the rapid freezing of cold water droplets of fog on to a cold surface.
A coating or sheet of ice so formed.
A film or slimy coating.
To freeze or congeal into hoarfrost.
The process of logging off.
A pain or ache in the head.
A nuisance or unpleasant problem.
definitions.example: The clumsy filing system has been a huge headache.

definitions.definition: An informal term of address, like man or dude.
definitions.example: Hey, holmes! What's up?

definitions.definition: A dwelling.
One’s native land; the place or country in which one dwells; the place where one’s ancestors dwell or dwelt.
The locality where a thing is usually found, or was first found, or where it is naturally abundant; habitat; seat.
definitions.example: the home of the pine

definitions.definition: A focus point.
(of animals) To return to its owner.
definitions.example: The dog homed.

definitions.definition: (always with "in on") To seek or aim for something.
definitions.example: The missile was able to home in on the target.

definitions.definition: A directory that contains a user's files.
A vertex or zenith.
A vertical geometrical figure; a perpendicular.
An individual slat in a set of vertical blinds.
A vertical component of a structure.
A vertical market.
definitions.example: We offer specialised accounting software targeting various verticals.

definitions.definition: Standing, pointing, or moving straight up or down; along the direction of a plumb line; perpendicular to something horizontal.
In a two-dimensional Cartesian co-ordinate system, describing the axis y oriented normal (perpendicular, at right angles) to the horizontal axis x.
In a three-dimensional co-ordinate system, describing the axis z oriented normal (perpendicular, orthogonal) to the basic plane xy.
Of or pertaining to vertical markets.
(wine tasting) Involving different vintages of the same wine type from the same winery.
Of an interval: having the two notes sound simultaneously.
To substantiate or prove the truth of something
To confirm or test the truth or accuracy of something
To affirm something formally, under oath
One who checks or verifies something.
One who makes a check mark.
The clerk who tallies cost of purchases and accepts payment.
definitions.example: There was a long line at the grocery store because the checker was so slow.

definitions.definition: One who hinders or stops something.
A natural attraction or feeling of kinship to a person or thing.
A family relationship through marriage of a relative (e.g. sister-in-law), as opposed to consanguinity (e.g. sister).
A kinsman or kinswoman of a such relationship; one who is affinal.
The fact of and manner in which something is related to another.
Any romantic relationship.
Any passionate love for something.
Resemblances between biological populations; resemblances that suggest that they are of a common origin, type or stock.
Structural resemblances between minerals; resemblances that suggest that they are of a common origin or type.
An attractive force between atoms, or groups of atoms, that contributes towards their forming bonds
The attraction between an antibody and an antigen
Tendency to keep a task running on the same processor in a symmetric multiprocessing operating system to reduce the frequency of cache misses
An automorphism of affine space.

(grammar, narrow sense) A word that can be used to refer to a person, animal, place, thing, phenomenon, substance, quality, or idea; one of the basic parts of speech in many languages, including English.

(grammar, now rare, broad sense) Either a word that can be used to refer to a person, animal, place, thing, phenomenon, substance, quality or idea, or a word that modifies or describes a previous word or its referent; a substantive or adjective, sometimes also including other parts of speech such as numeral or pronoun.
To convert a word to a noun.
The female genitalia.
definitions.example: Her dress was so short you could nearly see her fanny.

definitions.definition: The buttocks; arguably the most nearly polite of several euphemisms.
definitions.example: Children, sit down on your fannies, and eat your lunch.

definitions.definition: Sexual intercourse with a woman.
definitions.example: get some fanny tonight

definitions.definition: Women viewed as sexual objects.
definitions.example: This club is full of fanny.

definitions.definition: To overflow, as by water from excessive rainfall.
To cover or partly fill as if by a flood.
definitions.example: The floor was flooded with beer.

definitions.definition: To provide (someone or something) with a larger number or quantity of something than can easily be dealt with.
definitions.example: The station's switchboard was flooded with listeners making complaints.

definitions.definition: To paste numerous lines of text to (a chat system) in order to disrupt the conversation.
To bleed profusely, as after childbirth.
An act of flooding; a flood or gush.
Emotional overwhelm sometimes leading to a primal state of rage or panic.
To make resistant to damage from fire.
definitions.example: I hastily fireproofed my clothing somewhat by pouring a bucket of water over myself before dashing into the flames.

definitions.definition: Resistant to damage from fire.
definitions.example: The fireproof safe will protect documents inside for up to four hours in a standard house fire.

definitions.definition: To stock to an excessive degree.
To take a long position in.
To await, aspire, desire greatly (something to occur or to be true)
definitions.example: She longed for him to come back.

definitions.definition: To be appropriate to, to pertain or belong to.
To belong.
An attack made by moving the sword parallel to its length and landing with the point.
definitions.example: Pierre was a master swordsman, and could parry the thrusts of lesser men with barely a thought.

definitions.definition: A push, stab, or lunge forward (the act thereof.)
definitions.example: The cutpurse tried to knock her satchel from her hands, but she avoided his thrust and yelled, "Thief!"

definitions.definition: The force generated by propulsion, as in a jet engine.
definitions.example: Spacecraft are engineering marvels, designed to resist the thrust of liftoff, as well as the reverse pressure of the void.

definitions.definition: The primary effort; the goal.
definitions.example: Ostensibly, the class was about public health in general, but the main thrust was really sex education.

definitions.definition: To make advance with force.
definitions.example: We thrust at the enemy with our forces.

definitions.definition: To force something upon someone.
definitions.example: I asked her not to thrust the responsibility on me.

definitions.definition: To push out or extend rapidly or powerfully.
definitions.example: He thrust his arm into the icy stream and grabbed a wriggling fish, astounding the observers.

definitions.definition: A covering of linen, silk, or tapestry, spread over a dressing table in a chamber or dressing room.
The table covered by such a cloth; a dressing table.
Personal grooming; the process of washing, dressing and arranging the hair.
One's style of dressing: dress, outfit.
A dressing room.
A room or enclosed area containing a toilet: a bathroom or water closet.
definitions.example: Sorry, I was in the toilet.

definitions.definition: A small secondary lavatory having a toilet and sink but no bathtub or shower.
A chamber pot.
A fixture used for urination and defecation, particularly those with a large bowl and ring-shaped seat which use water to flush the waste material into a septic tank or sewer system.
definitions.example: My toilet backed up. Now the bathroom's flooded.

definitions.definition: A very shabby or dirty place.
To dress and groom oneself
To use the toilet
To assist another (a child etc.) in using the toilet
A pampered or coddled person.
An overly playful person; a trifler.
A self-indulgent person, fond of excess.
A lewd or immoral person, especially a prostitute.
To rove and ramble without restraint, rule, or limit; to revel; to play loosely; to frolic.
To waste or squander, especially in pleasure (most often with away).
definitions.example: The young man wantoned away his inheritance.

definitions.definition: To act wantonly; to be lewd or lascivious.
Undisciplined, unruly; not able to be controlled.
Playful, sportive; merry or carefree.
Lewd, immoral; sexually open, unchaste.
Capricious, reckless of morality, justice etc.; acting without regard for the law or the well-being of others; gratuitous.
Extravagant, unrestrained, excessive.
To separate into two or more pieces, to fracture or crack, by a process that cannot easily be reversed for reassembly.
definitions.example: If the vase falls to the floor, it might break.

definitions.definition: To divide (something, often money) into smaller units.
definitions.example: Can you break a hundred-dollar bill for me?

definitions.definition: To cause (a person or animal) to lose spirit or will; to crush the spirits of.
definitions.example: Her child's death broke Angela.

definitions.definition: To be crushed, or overwhelmed with sorrow or grief.
definitions.example: My heart is breaking.

definitions.definition: To interrupt; to destroy the continuity of; to dissolve or terminate.
definitions.example: I had won four games in a row, but now you've broken my streak of luck.

definitions.definition: To ruin financially.
definitions.example: The recession broke some small businesses.

definitions.definition: To violate, to not adhere to.
definitions.example: He broke his vows by cheating on his wife.

definitions.definition: (of a fever) To pass the most dangerous part of the illness; to go down, in terms of temperature.
definitions.example: Susan's fever broke at about 3 AM, and the doctor said the worst was over.

definitions.definition: (of a spell of settled weather) To end.
definitions.example: The forecast says the hot weather will break by midweek.

definitions.definition: (of a storm) To begin; to end.
definitions.example: Around midday the storm broke, and the afternoon was calm and sunny.

definitions.definition: (of morning, dawn, day etc.) To arrive.
definitions.example: Morning has broken.

definitions.definition: To render (a game) unchallenging by altering its rules or exploiting loopholes or weaknesses in them in a way that gives a player an unfair advantage.
definitions.example: Changing the rules to let white have three extra queens would break chess.

definitions.definition: To stop, or to cause to stop, functioning properly or altogether.
definitions.example: Did you two break the trolley by racing with it?

definitions.definition: To cause (a barrier) to no longer bar.
definitions.example: break a seal

definitions.definition: To destroy the arrangement of; to throw into disorder; to pierce.
definitions.example: The cavalry were not able to break the British squares.

definitions.definition: (of a wave of water) To collapse into surf, after arriving in shallow water.
To burst forth; to make its way; to come into view.
To interrupt or cease one's work or occupation temporarily.
definitions.example: Let's break for lunch.

definitions.definition: To interrupt (a fall) by inserting something so that the falling object does not (immediately) hit something else beneath.
definitions.example: He survived the jump out the window because the bushes below broke his fall.

definitions.definition: To disclose or make known an item of news, etc.
definitions.example: I don't know how to break this to you, but your cat is not coming back.

definitions.definition: (of a sound) To become audible suddenly.
To change a steady state abruptly.
definitions.example: His coughing broke the silence.

definitions.definition: To suddenly become.
definitions.example: The arrest was standard, when suddenly the suspect broke ugly.

definitions.definition: Of a male voice, to become deeper at puberty.
Of a voice, to alter in type due to emotion or strain: in men generally to go up, in women sometimes to go down; to crack.
definitions.example: His voice breaks when he gets emotional.

definitions.definition: To surpass or do better than (a specific number), to do better than (a record), setting a new record.
definitions.example: He broke the men's 100-meter record.

definitions.definition: (most often in the passive tense) To demote, to reduce the military rank of.
To end (a connection), to disconnect.
definitions.example: I couldn't hear a thing he was saying, so I broke the connection and called him back.

definitions.definition: (of an emulsion) To demulsify.
To counter-attack
To lay open, as a purpose; to disclose, divulge, or communicate.
To become weakened in constitution or faculties; to lose health or strength.
To fail in business; to become bankrupt.
To destroy the strength, firmness, or consistency of.

definitions.definition: To destroy the official character and standing of; to cashier; to dismiss.
To make an abrupt or sudden change; to change the gait.

definitions.definition: To fall out; to terminate friendship.
To terminate the execution of a program before normal completion.

The act by which something is broken.
A change of a vowel to a diphthong
A form of ornamentation in which groups of short notes are used instead of long ones

A paint containing an acrylic resin.

definitions.definition: A painting executed using such a paint.
A thick sheet of plastic.
A liquid coating system based on an acrylic resin

Containing an acrylic resin.
(sometimes derogatory) A person who is intellectual but generally introverted
(sometimes derogatory) One who has an intense, obsessive interest in something.

definitions.definition: An unattractive, socially awkward, annoying, undesirable, and/or boring, person; a dork.

definitions.definition: (post-1980s) A member of a subculture revolving around video games, fantasy and science fiction, comic books and assorted media.
An artificial intelligence researcher who believes that intelligence is too complicated (or computationally intractable) to be solved with the sorts of homogeneous system favoured by the "neats".

definitions.definition: Scurfy.
Rough, aggressive; powerful and successful.
Excellent, first-class.
A small, slender piece of wood or cardboard serving as a component of a match.

definitions.definition: (chiefly in the plural) Any similarly small and thin piece.

definitions.definition: The ability of the brain to record information or impressions with the facility of recalling them later at will.

definitions.definition: A record of a thing or an event stored and available for later use by the organism.

definitions.definition: The part of a computer that stores variable executable code or data (RAM) or unalterable executable code or default data (ROM).

definitions.definition: (of a material) which returns to its original shape when heated
definitions.example: Memory metal; memory plastic.

definitions.definition: A memorial.
(collective) A term of venery for a social group of elephants, normally called a herd.
A disparaging, belittling, or derogatory word or expression.
Disparaging, belittling or derogatory.
In the style of jazz.
Flashy or showy.
Lacking focus; jittery or jangly.
A day on which a festival, religious event, or national celebration is traditionally observed.

definitions.definition: A day declared free from work by the state or government.
A period of one or more days taken off work for leisure and often travel; often plural.
A period during which pupils do not attend their school; often plural; rarely used for students at university (usually: vacation).

definitions.definition: A period during which, by agreement, the usual payments are not made.

definitions.definition: A gap in coverage, e.g. of paint on a surface, or sonar imagery.
To take a period of time away from work or study.
To spend a period of time for travel.
(usually after possessive) holiday, vacation; time off work, especially if spent travelling away from home
The season around Christmas, Hanukkah, and New Year's Day.

definitions.definition: Make sad or dispirited.
To cast down.
Pigeons' dung used in tanning. See grainer.

definitions.definition: A large horse trained for battle and used by the cavalry (of a lighter build than a destrier)

definitions.definition: A large platter

Written works intended to give facts, or true accounts of real things and events. Often used attributively.

Frightening.

definitions.definition: Moral strength and resolve.

definitions.definition: Ivory.

Production; quantity produced, created, or completed.

Requiring arduous effort.

A coarse or stupid fellow.
Surly; annoyed; displeased; disgruntled.

A style of a capella vocal music, sung in four-part harmony, typically by a quartet of men.
A berserk (a crazed Norse warrior who fought in a frenzy).

Otherwise; at another time; in other circumstances; otherwise called.

The process of inducing the birth process.
An introduction.
Originally, a small ball placed in a container to cast a vote; now, by extension, a piece of paper or card used for this purpose, or some other means used to signify a vote.
The process of voting, especially in secret; a round of voting.
The total of all the votes cast in an election.
A list of candidates running for office; a ticket.
To vote or decide by ballot.
definitions.example: to ballot for a candidate

definitions.definition: To draw lots.
Decomposition into components in order to study (a complex thing, concept, theory etc.).
The result of such a process.
The mathematical study of functions, sequences, series, limits, derivatives and integrals.
Proof by deduction from known truths.
The process of breaking down a substance into its constituent parts, or the result of this process.
The analytical study of melodies, harmonies, sequences, repetitions, variations, quotations, juxtapositions, and surprises.
Psychoanalysis.
(grammar) A word that modifies a verb, adjective, other adverbs, or various other types of words, phrases, or clauses.
definitions.example: I often went outside hiking during my stay in Japan.

definitions.definition: To make into or become an adverb.
One of the simplest or essential parts or principles of which anything consists, or upon which the constitution or fundamental powers of anything are based.
definitions.example: Letters are the elements of written language.

definitions.definition: A small part of the whole.
definitions.example: an element of doubt;  an element of the picture

definitions.definition: The sky.
(with "the") Atmospheric forces such as strong winds and rains.
definitions.example: exposed to the elements

definitions.definition: A place or state of being that an individual or object is best suited to.
definitions.example: to be in one's element

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) The bread and wine taken at Holy Communion.
A group of people within a larger group having a particular common characteristic.
definitions.example: You sometimes find the hooligan element at football matches.

definitions.definition: A component in electrical equipment, often in the form of a coil, having a high resistance, thereby generating heat when a current is passed through it.
definitions.example: The element in this electric kettle can heat the water in under a minute.

definitions.definition: One of the conceptual objects in a markup language, usually represented in text by tags.
Outdoor weather, such as wind or rain.
The basic tenets of an area of knowledge.
The bread and wine of the Eucharist.
An instance of moving to live in another place for a while.
Seasonal moving of animals, as mammals, birds or fish, especially between breeding and non-breeding areas.
Movement in general.
definitions.example: The migration of lead from a can to the food inside it can cause lead poisoning.

definitions.definition: Instance of changing a platform from an environment to another one.
The movement of cells in particular directions to specific locations.
An elevation of land of considerable dimensions rising more or less abruptly, forming a conspicuous figure in the landscape, usually having a small extent of surface at its summit.
definitions.example: We spent the weekend hiking in the mountains.

definitions.definition: Something very large in size or quantity; a huge amount; a great heap.
definitions.example: He was a real mountain of a man, standing seven feet tall.

definitions.definition: Of, belonging to, relating to, or found on a mountain; like a mountain in size; (of living things) growing or living on a mountain.
definitions.example: some mountain ranges; the high mountain top; a scenic mountain view; the mountain lodge; the leaves of mountain cranberries; a mountain goat

definitions.definition: A difficult task or challenge.
A woman's large breast.
The twenty-first Lenormand card.
Wine made from grapes that grow on a mountain.
A wandering troop or gang; especially, a clan or tribe of a nomadic people (originally Tatars) migrating from place to place for the sake of pasturage, plunder, etc.; a predatory multitude.
A large number of people.
definitions.example: We were beset by a horde of street vendors who thought we were tourists and would buy their cheap souvenirs.

definitions.definition: To travel en masse, to flock
To become sleepy or weary.
To make sleepy or weary.
To become bored or impatient (with).
definitions.example: I tire of this book.

definitions.definition: To bore.
A fruit of the coconut palm (not a true nut), Cocos nucifera, having a fibrous husk surrounding a large seed.
A hard-shelled seed of this fruit, having white flesh and a fluid-filled central cavity.
The edible white flesh of this fruit.
The coconut palm.
A Hispanic or dark-skinned person who acts “white” (Caucasian), alluding to the fact that a coconut is brown on the outside and white on the inside.
A black person who thinks or acts "white" (European).
A Pacific islander.
A female breast.
The human head (often used in cricket broadcasts when a ball hits or nearly hits a batsman on the head).
To become or remain attached; to adhere.
definitions.example: The tape will not stick if it melts.

definitions.definition: To jam; to stop moving.
definitions.example: The lever sticks if you push it too far up.

definitions.definition: To tolerate, to endure, to stick with.
To persist.
definitions.example: His old nickname stuck.

definitions.definition: Of snow, to remain frozen on landing.
To remain loyal; to remain firm.
definitions.example: Just stick to your strategy, and you will win.

definitions.definition: To hesitate, to be reluctant; to refuse (in negative phrases).
To be puzzled (at something), have difficulty understanding.
To cause difficulties, scruples, or hesitation.
To attach with glue or as if by gluing.
definitions.example: Stick the label on the jar.

definitions.definition: To place, set down (quickly or carelessly).
definitions.example: Stick your bag over there and come with me.

definitions.definition: To press (something with a sharp point) into something else.
definitions.example: The balloon will pop when I stick this pin in it.

definitions.definition: To fix on a pointed instrument; to impale.
definitions.example: to stick an apple on a fork

definitions.definition: To adorn or deck with things fastened on as by piercing.
To perform (a landing) perfectly.
definitions.example: Once again, the world champion sticks the dismount.

definitions.definition: To propagate plants by cuttings.
definitions.example: Stick cuttings from geraniums promptly.

definitions.definition: (joinery) To run or plane (mouldings) in a machine, in contradistinction to working them by hand. Such mouldings are said to be stuck.
To bring to a halt; to stymie; to puzzle.
definitions.example: to stick somebody with a hard problem

definitions.definition: To impose upon; to compel to pay; sometimes, to cheat.
To have sexual intercourse with.
To stand pat: to cease taking any more cards and finalize one's hand.
Unable to move.
definitions.example: Can you shift this gate? I think it’s stuck.

definitions.definition: Unable to progress with a task.
definitions.example: I’m totally stuck on this question in the test.

definitions.definition: No longer functioning, frozen up, frozen.
definitions.example: There are several ways to close a stuck program.

definitions.definition: Any of a range of colours having the longest wavelengths, 670 nm, of the visible spectrum; a primary additive colour for transmitted light: the colour obtained by subtracting green and blue from white light using magenta and yellow filters; the colour of blood, ripe strawberries, etc.
A revolutionary socialist or (most commonly) a Communist; (usually capitalized) a Bolshevik, a supporter of the Bolsheviks in the Russian Civil War.
One of the 15 red balls used in snooker, distinguished from the colours.
Red wine.
A redshank.
An Amerind.
The drug secobarbital; a capsule of this drug.
A red light (a traffic signal)
(beverages) red lemonade
One of the three color charges for quarks.
Chili con carne (usually in the phrase "bowl of red")
The redfish or red drum, Sciaenops ocellatus, a fish with reddish fins and scales.
Having red as its color.
definitions.example: The girl wore a red skirt.

definitions.definition: (of hair) Having an orange-brown or orange-blond colour; ginger.
definitions.example: Her hair had red highlights.

definitions.definition: (of a card) Of the hearts or diamonds suits. Compare black
definitions.example: I got two red queens, and he got one of the black queens.

definitions.definition: (often capitalized) Supportive of, related to, or dominated by a political party or movement represented by the color red:
Amerind; relating to Amerindians or First Nations
Of the lower-frequency region of the (typically visible) part of the electromagnetic spectrum which is relevant in the specific observation.
Having a color charge of red.
That which is produced or occurs as a result of an event or process.
definitions.example: A quality automobile is the outcome of the work of skilled engineers and thousands of workers.

definitions.definition: The result of a random trial. An element of a sample space.
definitions.example: Three is a possible outcome of tossing a six-sided die.

definitions.definition: The anticipated or desired results or evidence of a learning experience (often used in the phrase learning outcomes).
definitions.example: The outcomes of this course are outlined in your syllabus.

definitions.definition: The scoreline; the result.
